name: Micronesia
headline: Micronesia ROTATION-BACKFILL CONFIRMATION at 48.4 / Functional. Pre-vote baseline check before May 20 UNGA ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion follow-up resolution. FSM is co-sponsor of Vanuatu-led resolution (alongside Marshall Islands, Palau, Netherlands, Kenya, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Barbados, Jamaica, Philippines, Burkina Faso). FSM President Wesley Simina UNGA 80 address: 'Security Council lacks legitimacy when regions suffering its decisions most...hold the least sway.' Climate-leadership multilateral-engagement consistent. Sub-threshold positive (co-sponsor signal); hold first-baseline pending May 20 vote outcome.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. FSM Simina UNGA 80 address articulates clear awareness of existential-risk asymmetry.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. UNGA co-sponsor signal demonstrates multilateral-engagement-as-action; awaiting May 20 vote.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Existential-equity framing (drowning atolls) is canonical small-island-state EQU advocacy.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. UN Security Council reform advocacy = ACC-positive institutional posture.

INT.rationale: Sustained. I1 consistency-under-pressure: small-state climate-leadership posture consistent across UNGA cycles.

evidence.finding: May 20 UNGA ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion follow-up resolution co-sponsors include Micronesia, Marshall Islands, Palau, Netherlands, Kenya, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Barbados, Jamaica, Philippines, Burkina Faso (Vanuatu-led).

FSM President Wesley Simina UNGA 80 address: 'Security Council lacks legitimacy when regions suffering its decisions most...hold the least sway. For Micronesia's low-lying communities, this gap threatens not just equity, but our physical existence.'
April 22 2026: Micronesia pushes UN reform; Security Council 'no longer adequately represents today's world.'
ICJ follow-up resolution = test of climate leadership at UN.

keyEvidence: FSM co-sponsor of May 20 UNGA ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion resolution.
FSM President Simina UNGA 80 'drowning atolls' / Security Council legitimacy address.
Sub-threshold positive; pre-vote hold; reassess post-May 20 outcome.
rationale: Rotation-backfill first-baseline confirmation at 48.4 with pre-vote hold. FSM's UNGA co-sponsorship + Simina address articulate clear awareness/empathy/equity posture, but pre-vote conservative-direction protocol holds dimensional weight until May 20 vote outcome (passage + margin). Reassess post-vote; potential sub-threshold positive (+0.3-0.5) if resolution passes with strong majority. Math: (3+3+3+2.75+3+2.75+3+3)/8 = 23.5/8 = 2.9375 → ((2.9375-1)/4)*100 = 48.44 → 48.4.
whyHeadline: Micronesia first-baseline 48.4; pre-May-20 UNGA ICJ Climate vote co-sponsor.

dominantDimension.note: AWR carries the strongest co-sponsor + Simina-address signal (existential-risk articulation).

nextForwardSignal.label: UNGA ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion follow-up resolution vote

watches: FSM Security Council reform advocacy continuation
boundaryWatchResolution: Not boundary-sensitive. 48.4 sits 7.4pt above Developing/Functional floor.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. First-baseline confirmation, pre-vote hold.
